## Purpose 
uniformly use `cos_zenith` throughout ClimaCoupler

closes #1281
title: rename cos_zenith_angle -> cos_zenith

body:
Switching to PartitionedStateFluxes introduced a bug where insolation was not updated. This is because the correct `update_sim!` method was not being called.

This commit fixes that. In parallel, I also fixed the update of insolation in ClimaAtmos so that it is automatically updated every time RRTGMP is called.

https://github.com/CliMA/ClimaAtmos.jl/pull/3788

As I discuss in that issue, I think the only reason for updating the insolation tuple in ClimaCoupler is to do it more often that the radiation timestep. I think this is because the `cos_zenith_angle` is one of the exchanged field. So, if we remove `cos_zenith_angle` from the exchange fields, we can remove this specialized method in `climaatmos.jl` and use the default one in `FieldExchanger`.
title: Fix insolation update

body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
Instead of storing `F_turb_energy` in the coupler fields, store `F_lhf` and `F_shf` individually. This allows us to provide a more accurate flux partition to the land models. No extra computation is required because SurfaceFluxes.jl already computes and returns LHF and SHF individually.

closes #600

## Content
- [x] replace `F_turb_energy` in CoupledSimulation fields with `F_shf`, `F_lhf`
- [x] in land model `update_turbulent_fluxes!` methods, access `F_shf` and `F_lhf` individually and update the model with each
- [x] in PrescribedSeaIce, SlabOcean `update_turbulent_fluxes!`, combine `F_shf + F_lhf` and use this sum to fill `sim.integrator.p.F_turb_energy`
title: exchange LHF, SHF individually
